pi_items.source: Task description explicitly requests 'Safest' security level.

Source code analysis of Tor Browser's omni.ja (SecurityLevel.sys.mjs) confirms that security_slider value 1 corresponds to 'high' (Safest), while 4 corresponds to 'low' (Standard). The task's verifier script incorrectly maps 4 to Safest.

Review of Firefox privacy configurations (e.g., arkenfox user.js) confirms that setting browser.sessionstore.privacy_level to 2 prevents session data from being saved, network.http.speculative-parallel-limit to 0 disables speculative connections, and network.prefetch-next to false disables link prefetching.
Task description explicit instructions and standard Firefox/Tor privacy settings.

privileged_info_summary: Analysis of the Tor Browser source code (omni.ja/SecurityLevel.sys.mjs) reveals a critical flaw in the task's verification logic: the 'Safest' security level actually corresponds to a browser.security_level.security_slider value of 1, whereas a value of 4 corresponds to 'Standard' (lowest security). The task metadata and verifier script incorrectly expect a value of 4 for 'Safest'. The other required preference changes (network.prefetch-next=false, browser.sessionstore.privacy_level=2, and network.http.speculative-parallel-limit=0) correctly map to established Firefox privacy hardening practices.
